text.title: Fine-tuning Technique Selection
text.summary: Decision matrix for choosing between full fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, and DoRA based on GPU VRAM, dataset size, and task complexity; rank selection guide; target_modules by model family.

rule.statement: Select technique by available VRAM: <12GB → QLoRA; 16-24GB → LoRA; 80GB+ → full fine-tuning; no GPU → OpenAI API fine-tuning.
rule.rationale: Wrong technique selection causes OOM at training start. QLoRA reduces a 7B model's VRAM requirement from 28GB to 6-8GB with minimal quality loss.

list.item: Full fine-tuning: 80GB+ VRAM, best quality, highest cost, risk of catastrophic forgetting
LoRA: 16-24GB VRAM, good quality, fast iteration, adapter-based — preserves base capabilities
QLoRA: 8-12GB VRAM, good quality, 4-bit base model + fp16 adapters, best for consumer GPUs
DoRA: 16-24GB VRAM, better than LoRA on style tasks, decomposes magnitude and direction separately
OpenAI API: no GPU needed, good quality, fast, no infra — see fine-tuning-openai-basics methodology

rule.statement: Start with rank 16 for most tasks; increase to 32-64 only if validation loss plateaus and you have more than 5k examples.
rule.rationale: Higher rank means more trainable parameters and slower training. Rank 16 provides sufficient capacity for the majority of format/style adaptation tasks.

list.item: rank 8: minimal memory, simple tasks (classification, extraction), prototyping
rank 16: general fine-tuning, instruction following — default choice
rank 32: complex tasks requiring nuanced adaptation
rank 64+: rarely needed; diminishing returns above rank 32 for most tasks

rule.statement: Always include at minimum q_proj and v_proj; add k_proj, o_proj, and MLP layers (gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj) for complex tasks.
rule.rationale: q_proj and v_proj are the most impactful attention components for style adaptation. MLP layers matter for knowledge-intensive tasks.

list.item: LLaMA/Mistral: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj, o_proj, gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj

rule.statement: Verify trainable parameter count immediately after applying LoRA — if it is 0, stop and fix target_modules before starting training.
rule.rationale: Wrong module names cause 0 trainable parameters; training proceeds silently for hours producing a model identical to the base.
